title: How Kenyan Creators Get Paid Internationally: The Minimum Viable Setup That Actually Works
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: As a Kenyan creator, you're facing a daunting challenge: how to get paid for your digital products internationally with PayPal and Stripe restricting your access due to 'geographic restrictions.' You're not alone. Thousands of creatives worldwide are struggling with the same issue. But there's a solution that can help you bypass these restrictions and start selling your ebooks, courses, or software to anyone, anywhere in the world: Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle. In this post, we'll show you the minimum viable setup that actually works to get you paid internationally, starting today.
What You Can Actually Get Done Today

The geographic exclusion that creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh face from conventional payment platforms is not the result of individual decisions — it is systemic. PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad all operate within the traditional financial system, which means they are subject to the same correspondent banking relationships, compliance requirements, and risk frameworks that make international money movement difficult in certain corridors. Blockchain-based payments operate outside this system entirely. A USDT transaction from a buyer in Germany to a seller in Lagos does not pass through any correspondent bank. It does not require a compliance officer to approve the corridor. It settles peer-to-peer, on-chain, in under 60 seconds.
The Minimum Viable Setup That Works

To get paid internationally, you'll need a payment processor that allows you to accept cryptocurrency payments. NOWPayments is a game-changer in this space, offering 300+ coins and no KYC requirements. With Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle, you'll get instant automated delivery, global reach, and fully automated payment processing. This is the minimum viable setup that works for Kenyan creators like you.
Skipping the Steps That Do Not Matter Yet

GitHub Pages provides free, reliable hosting for static sites with no server management required. A digital store built on GitHub Pages with the Core Shop Engine has no monthly hosting cost, scales automatically to any traffic level, and can be deployed from anywhere in the world. The combination of free hosting, crypto payments through NOWPayments, and automated delivery through the Core Shop Engine creates a store infrastructure with a total cost structure that is fundamentally different from platform-based alternatives. There are no percentage fees on sales, no monthly subscription costs, and no platform that can decide to delist your products based on content policy changes.
Kenyan creators can bypass PayPal and Stripe restrictions to sell digital products internationally with Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle.
Getting Your First Transaction Live

To get your first transaction live, follow these steps: set up a NOWPayments account, configure Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle to use NOWPayments, create a digital product and add it to your store, and test the payment flow. This is a straightforward process that can be completed in a few hours. By getting your first transaction live, you'll be able to test your product, payment flow, and customer support.
What to Do in the Next 24 Hours

In the next 24 hours, focus on promoting your digital product to your audience. Use social media, email marketing, and other channels to get the word out. By promoting your product, you'll be able to drive traffic to your store and increase the chances of getting your first sale. Don't forget to track your analytics to see what's working and what's not, and make adjustments accordingly.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
NOWPayments offers 300+ coins and no KYC requirements, making it a game-changer for cryptocurrency payments.

Here's what you need to do to get paid internationally: Create a digital product that solves a real problem for your audience.
